result:
The experiment is pre-registered in `runs/pending_experiment.json` and the figure passes figcheck. The harness can now take over.

**What I designed — exp 33: "Sensor-sharpness nuisance randomization"** (category: augmentation, the training-data family)

**Why this pivot.** All 7 consecutive reverts (exps 26–32) attacked either architecture (capacity, stride-8 tap, dispatcher, neural map) or the confidence operating point (calibration, per-regime abstention — exp 32's designed fix also failed at 1078 m vs the 797.91 m champion). The training-data family is absent from that streak and is 4-for-4 kept across the project (exps 7, 17, 20, 25). The iter-12 probes narrowed the bottleneck to ranking quality (true cell is top-1 on only 2% of eval crops; decode and rotation are ruled out) and left exactly one measured, untested lever: crops that skip PIL's rotation resampling come out crisper than anything the model trained on, and its median error nearly **doubles** on them (983→1668 m). That proves the descriptor leans heavily on resampling-blur texture — a signal that is identical everywhere on the map and carries zero location information, so every unit of sensitivity spent on it steals capacity from the layout features that ranking needs.

**The one change** (confined to `sample_epoch` in `model/train.py`): each training crop rolls a random sharpness — with p=0.25 its heading snaps to 0° so it arrives pin-sharp (the C4 fan supplies the other three crisp orientations), and independently with p=0.5 it gets an extra Gaussian blur with σ~U(0.4, 1.4) px. Training sharpness then spans crisper-than-eval to softer-than-eval, so blur stops being a usable cue — the same mechanism that made exp 17 a keep, on this newly measured axis. The confidence calibration path deliberately stays eval-matched (no jitter), so the coverage confound that vetoed exps 28–31 shouldn't contaminate the comparison.

**Pre-registered prediction:** worst-case §6 median drops ~5–15% to ≈680–760 m if the shortcut is taxing ranking, with the crisp-crop pathology collapsing as the confirming signature; ≈800–850 m and revert if invariance just makes the fit harder. I also updated the memory note so future iterations know this lever is now under test rather than untested.

tool_input.command: ls model/ pipeline/ 2>/dev/null; cat /FROZEN 2>/dev/null || cat FROZEN 2>/dev/null
tool_input.description: List model and pipeline files, show FROZEN list

tool_input.command: sqlite3 experiments.sqlite "SELECT id FROM experiments WHERE arch_json IS NOT NULL ORDER BY id DESC LIMIT 1;"; sqlite3 experiments.sqlite "SELECT arch_json FROM experiments WHERE arch_json IS NOT NULL ORDER BY id DESC LIMIT 1;" | python3 -c "import json,sys; [print(s.get('train_only',False), '|', s['name'], '|', s['detail'][:100]) for s in json.load(sys.stdin)['stages']]"
tool_input.description: Get last experiment's architecture stage names

tool_input.command: sqlite3 experiments.sqlite "SELECT arch_json FROM experiments WHERE arch_json IS NOT NULL ORDER BY id DESC LIMIT 1;" > /tmp/last_arch.json && sqlite3 experiments.sqlite "SELECT id, title FROM experiments WHERE arch_json IS NOT NULL ORDER BY id DESC LIMIT 1;"
tool_input.description: Dump last arch_json to temp file
